- 0.2. The first examiner will create a worksheet (merged template) that will include DBQ Hypertension and DBQ Medical Opinion because they are the provider to evaluate the hypertension condition and provide a medical opinion as requested in the 2507.
- 0.3. The first examiner completes both the DBQ Hypertension and DBQ Medical Opinion then signs and submits the worksheet, which is sent to VBMS.
- 0.5. The second examiner will create a worksheet (merged template) that will include DBQ Derm Scars and DBQ Medical Opinion because they are the provider to evaluate the derm scars condition and provide a medical opinion as requested in the 2507.
- 0.6. The second examiner completes both the DBQ Derm scars and DBQ Medical Opinion then signs and submits the worksheet, which is sent to VBMS.

> **NOTE:** The process will be repeated for 2507s that required any number of exams with separate examiners who all needed to complete separate DBQ medical opinions.

### CPWM Exam Management Functions

#### Unsigned C&amp;P Worksheets

To view the list of C&amp;P worksheets needing a signature, users go to the Tools menu (Figure 2-190) and select My Unsigned C&amp;P Worksheets…

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-190. CAPRI Tools Menu—My Unsigned Worksheets Selection.

#### Unsigned Templates

Selecting My Unsigned C&amp;P Worksheets from the Tools menu (Figure 2-190) displays a list of unsigned templates (Figure 2-191).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-191. Unsigned Worksheets View.

This window lists forms that have been started by examiners but not completed. Information provided includes ID (first letter of last name + last four digits of SSN), patient name, worksheet names, author, and status.

Selecting the **My Unsigned** button displays **the user’s own** unsigned forms.

Selecting the **All Unsigned** button displays **all** unsigned forms.

Selecting the **Review Pending** button (Figure 2-192) displays the forms that were sent for review. Only users who hold the DVBAB CPWM Reviewer Security Key have access to documents waiting for review. All users can see the documents, but only reviewers can open and work with the documents.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-192. Unsigned Templates—Review Pending Selection.

The user selects the form to complete by double-clicking it. If it is clicked only once, the **Display Form** and **Delete Form** buttons are enabled. The user selects **Display Form** . The user must hold the required Security Key to delete another user’s template.

#### Uncosigned C&amp;P Worksheets

To view the list of the of C&amp;P worksheets that need their cosignature, users can go to the Tools menu then select My Uncosigned Documents (Figure 2-193).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-193. CAPRI Tools Menu—My Uncosigned Documents Selection.

If a document has been sent to the user for cosignature, then the user is able to select the document from the list, view the report, add any addendum, and then cosign the document from CAPRI (Figure 2-194)

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-194 Uncosigned Documents Window

#### Manage C&amp;P Exam Request Report Management

The Manage C&amp;P Exam Requests option available on the Tools Menu when a patient is selected can be used to reopen a released exam, release an open exam, change the status of an Exam Request, or edit the report of an exam that has already been signed and completed in CAPRI or AMIE.

#### Reopen a Released Exam

To reopen a released exam, the user clicks the exam request date, selects the exam name under Exams, and clicks the Re-open this request button. The user selects Yes to the warning message to reopen the exam, as shown in (Figure 2-195).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-195. Reopen Released Exam Prompt.

#### Change Exam Status

Exam status can be changed to Open, Completed, Canceled by MAS, Canceled by RO, or Transferred out by using **Option Manage C&amp;P Exam Request** functionality available on the Tools Menu. This opens the C&amp;P Exam Request Report Management screen.

The user clicks on the date then clicks on the desired exam. Then, the user clicks the Edit Selected Exam button to open the exam as shown in Figure 2-196).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-196. Manage C&amp;P Exam Request View.

The user clicks on the down arrow button on the **STATUS** box to mark the new status for the selected exam. Exam Location, Date of the Exam and Examining Provider may also be edited. The user clicks the **Save Changes** button (Figure 2-197) and confirms changes by selecting **Yes** on the Confirm message box.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-197. Manage C&amp;P Exam Request Selection.

#### Edit a Released Exam

After a Released Exam is reopened (Figure 2-197), the user selects it by highlighting it in the Exams window and selects Edit Selected Exam. The Exam appears in the Report Text window for editing (Figure 2-198).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-198. Edit Released Exam Option.

#### Miscellaneous CPWM Functionalities

The Tools menu (Figure 2-199) has some options that are not discussed elsewhere in the User Manual.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-199. CAPRI Tools Menu

#### View AMIE Worksheets

Selecting **AMIE Worksheets** takes the user to a C&amp;P website that provides Index to Disability Examination Worksheets. Selecting a worksheet displays instructions for using the worksheets.

#### Bring Exam Template to Front

If a user is working on the C&amp;P worksheets tab and loses template focus, or if the template happens to be stuck in the background, users can go to the Tools menu and select Bring Exam Template to front to bring the template back into focus.

#### External Application Access

Several links to additional web based applications are available from the External Applications menu as shown in (Figure 2-200).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-200. CAPRI Tools Menu—External Applications Option

### Vocational Rehabilitation Tab

#### Overview

Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment (VR&amp;E) employees require the ability to refer a Veteran to a medical center for the purpose of specific types of exams or opinions in order to determine VR&amp;E benefits.

This feature enables users to create an electronic request for medical services, track those requests and print reports. A consult is created outside the CAPRI application. The request can be linked to the consult by a VHA Coordinator. Notifications are sent to both VR&amp;E Counselor (via Outlook) and VHA Coordinator (via MAILMAN) when the 28-8861 form status is updated. A nightly background job monitors consults that are linked to the 28-8861 file.

#### User Setup

#### Security Key Assignment

To activate the VocRehab tab for a particular user, depending on their role, the following security keys need to be given:

DVBA CAPRI WORKSHEET

DVBAB CPWM REQUIRE REVIEW

DVBAB CPWM DISALLOW REVIEW

DVBAB CPWM OPTIONAL REVIEW

DVBAB CPWM REVIEWER

**DVBA CAPRI VRE\_COUNSELOR** – This security key is given to the VBA/VR&amp;E on the **CLAIMS** system so that the VocRehab tab is available to the user. This key must also be given to the VHA Coordinator role on the **local VistA system** for the VocRehab tab to be available to the user.

**DVBA CAPRI VHA\_COORDINATOR** – The VHA Coordinator role needs this security key assigned on the **local VistA system** to allow the ability to assign a consult to a Medical Services Request. The **A** **ssign Consults** button located on the **Medical Services Requests** screen is to be only available to the VHA Coordinator role.

#### VocRehab Tab Functionality

The VocRehab tab provides access to the Medical Services Requests screen (Figure 2-201). The user must have the proper security key (DVBA CAPRI VRE COUNSELOR) for this tab to be visible.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-201. VocRehab Tab View.

#### Add a New Request (VBA/VR&amp;E Counselor Role)

**Step 1** – The user logs into CAPRI, looks up the Veteran using the **Patient Selector** , and moves to the **VocRehab** tab (Figure 2-201).

**Step 2** – The user clicks on the **Add** **N** **ew Request** button to add a new Chapter 31, VA Form 28-8861 request (Medical Services Request).

The field explanations on the screenshot shown in (Figure 2-202) are listed below:

**Patient Name:** This is the name of the patient selected from the **Patient Selector** screen. This is a read-only field and is not editable.

**VA Claim#:** If a VA Claim Number exists in the New Patient file for the selected Veteran, it is displayed here. This is a read-only field and is not editable.

**Request Date:** This is the date the request is initiated. This is set by the system clock and is not a user editable field.

**Anticipated Date of Rehabilitation:** This is a required field noting a future date when rehabilitation is anticipated to be complete.

**Regional Office:** This is the requestor’s Regional Office and is derived from the user’s log in.

**Point of Contact:** This button displays a screen with the Point of Contact information. This is the user who entered the Medical Services Request

**Medical Services Requested:** Users must choose from a predefined list of medical services. If the medical service resides outside of this list, **Other** can be selected. The **Other Description** field is then enabled.

**Other Description:** This field is enabled if **Other** is chosen in the Medical Services Requested field. This is a required field if enabled.

**Reason for Referral:** This is a required field. It is a free text field that is enabled for a maximum of 50 characters to briefly describe the reason a referral is being made.

**Rehabilitation Objectives:** This is a required field. It is a free text field that is enabled for a maximum of 100 characters to allow input of rehabilitation objectives.

**Comments / Notes:** This is a free text field allowing for brief or long descriptions relating to this medical request. The field is always editable unless the Request for Medical Service has been cancelled or completed.

**Preferred Schedule Date:** If the Veteran needs to be seen by a certain date, a Preferred Schedule Date can be entered.

**Preferred Schedule Date Reason:** This field cannot be blank if a Preferred Schedule Date is specified. One needs to explain the reason for specifying a Preferred Schedule Date.

**S** **end Medical Services Request** button. This button submits all the data input and initiates the Medical Services Request

**C** **ancel** button: This button closes the form without saving a “new” Medical Services Request.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-202. VocRehab Add New Medical Services Request.

#### Edit Request (VBA/VR&amp;E Counselor and VHA Coordinator Roles)

**Step 1** – The user logs into CAPRI, looks up the Veteran using the **Patient Selector** , and moves to the **VocRehab** tab.

**Step 2** – The user clicks on the date of the request they would like to edit and clicks **Edit Request** button to edit a Chapter 31, VA Form 28-8861 request (Medical Services Request).

The fields for editing a Medical Services Request and adding one are identical and have the following exceptions:

**Consult Selected:** This field is read-only. It displays “No Consults Found” if a consult has NOT been assigned to the Medical Services Request yet or it displays the Date, Time, and Consult Name of the consult assigned to this Medical Services Request

**Assign Consult** button: This button opens the **Assign Consult** dialog box and allows the user to associate a consult with a request. It is only selectable if the user has the security key of **DVBA CAPRI VHA\_COORDINATOR (VHA Coordinator role)**

**Update Medical Services Request** button. This updates the request.

**Cancel Medical Services Request** button: This button cancels the Medical Services Request. The request remains in the database for historical purposes, but its status shows as **Cancelled** . This button is not available on a new request, or if a consult has already been assigned.

Once a consult has been assigned to this **Medical Services Request** , the only field that can be edited is the **Comments / Notes** field. All other fields are read-only.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-203 VocRehab Edit Medical Services Request.

#### Point of Contact

This screen (Figure 2-204) displays the Point of Contact information for the Medical Services Request. It is derived from the user’s credentials that logged into CAPRI and entered the request.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-204. VocRehab Medical Services Point of Contact View.

#### Assign Consults

This screen (Figure 2-205) is only visible to users who have a security key of **DVBA CAPRI VHA\_COORDINATOR** . The user selects a consult that was previously created for the Veteran in CPRS to assign to the Medical Services Request under the VocRehab tab. This assignment must take place in order for the consult status to be tracked. Only one consult can be assigned to one Medical Services Request.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-205. VocRehab Medical Services Assign Consults View.

#### Cancel Request

The cancel request screen (Figure 2-206), allows a user to cancel a Medical Services Request. This option is only available if a consult has not already been linked (assigned) to this request. A request cancellation reason must be selected from the choices displayed on the screen. If **Other** is selected as a reason, an explanation must be entered in the **Cancellation Explanation** field.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-206. VocRehab Medical Services Cancellation Request.

#### Notification Messages

Upon specific events, MAILMAN messages are sent to VHA Coordinators who must be a member of the MAILMAN mail group: DVBA VR VOCREHAB PERSONNEL and the VR&amp;E Counselor(s) receive email notifications via Outlook. These notifications are sent for the following status changes to the Medical Services Request:

**New Status** : This message reads: **Chapter 31 Referral for Medical Services New. **** This notification is only sent to the **VHA Coordinator** via MAILMAN

**Pending Status** : This message reads: **Chapter 31 Referral for Medical Services Pending.** **In CAPRI, once the VHA Coordinator/or designated person links the consult request to the original Chapter 31, VA Form 28-8861 request, the status of the consult in CAPRI is then changed to **Pending** . Both the VHA Coordinator and VR&amp;E Counselor receive notification messages

**Completed Status** : This message reads: **Chapter 31 Referral for Medical Services Completed **** Both the VHA Coordinator and VR&amp;E Counselor receive notification messages.

**Cancelled Status** : This message reads: **Chapter 31 Referral for Medical Services Cancelled**** Both the VHA Coordinator and VR&amp;E Counselor receive notification messages.

(Figure 2-207), is an example of a New Status MAILMAN message the VHA Coordinator would receive.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-207. VocRehab Notification Message.

#### Aging Notification Messages

Aging notification messages (Figure 2-208), are sent when a New Medical Services Request age five, six, and seven days. Five- and six-day notifications are sent alerting the VHA Coordinator via Mailman to link a consult to the Medical Services Request. Seven-day aging notifications are sent to the VR&amp;E Counselor via Outlook alerting them that no consult has been linked to the form yet.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-208. VocRehab Aged Notification Message.

### Additional CAPRI Functionality

#### CAPRI Remote Functionality

CAPRI Remote users access CAPRI through the Claims system, which runs on the VHA Forum hardware. The server for those users should be set to CLAIMS server and the port number.

CAPRI Remote users outside of VBA normally obtain an access code from the Office of Information (OI) support staff, not from the local field site. Most VBA users are CAPRI Remote users. Most VHA users are local site users. If a user starts CAPRI and does not successfully connect to a VistA system within 90 seconds, CAPRI automatically shuts down.

When users log into CAPRI remotely, CAPRI alerts users when no email account is set up.

#### VistA Terminal

The “Attachmate Reflections Secure Shell” application replaces the telnet window. CAPRI Remote users can launch a VistA Terminal session by selecting the VistA button to log into the local VistA system site they are assigned to. A dialog box is displayed when the VistA button is clicked that provides the user the ability to choose between connecting using the secure shell application or telnet. The default is set to secure shell application.

> **NOTE:** Local CAPRI users do not have access to the VistA Terminal from CAPRI and the VistA button is not available.

#### Switch Sites – On the File Menu

The **Switch Sites** option, available on the File Menu (Figure 2-209), is active if the user is accessing a VistA system with remote capability. If the selection is active, the user may have other VistA accounts they can connect to without re-authenticating.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-209. CAPRI File Menu—Switch Sites Selection.

CAPRI Remote users can use this option to switch from one site to another without logging out of CAPRI. This option is only available when users are logged into the claims server with remote access. Users can access the **Switch Sites…** option from the File menu. After **Switch Sites…** is selected, the user is prompted with the site list to allow them to choose the site to which they would like to switch.

#### Check Remote Connections

**Check Remote Connections,** found on the Help menu, is available to users with @ or U level FileMan access (Figure 2-210). This provides a utility to scan CAPRI remote sites to discover any connectivity issues.

To use this function, the user selects the connections to scan in the left list box or clicks **Select All** and then **Run Selected** . Any connection problems are listed in the **Could not connect to** field. This does not mean that the site is down nationwide; it means that the current user cannot access the site, which could be due to several reasons.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-210. Validate Remote Connections View.

#### Send to VBA eFolder– On the File Menu

This feature creates an interface between CAPRI and the Claims Evidence Webservice. It allows CAPRI users to electronically transmit CAPRI-generated Veteran-related documents that ***can be printed today*** to the VBA eFolder. The Send to VBA eFolder option (Figure 2-211) is available within the **File** menu and from the **Send to VBA eFolder** button within the Clinical Documents Report Builder application. In order to send clinical documents to VBA eFolder users must hold the **DVBA CAPRI CLIN DOC-EFOLDER** security key and fall under one of the following user categories:

- **VBA** – All VBA Users should have access to upload clinical documents to the VBMS eFolder.
- **VHA** – Limited, only specific roles should have access to upload clinical documents to the VBMS eFolder.
- **Spokane** – All providers at the Spokane site should have access to upload clinical documents to the VBMS eFolder.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-211. CAPRI File Menu – Send to VBA eFolder Selection.

Documents within the following tabs of CAPRI have the capability of being transmitted to the VBA eFolder via the **File&gt;Send to VBA eFolder** (Figure 2-211).

**Reports** tab (patient related reports only)

**Admin** tab

**Health Summaries** tab

**Clinical Documents** tab

**Report Builder** **(see Section 2.8.2)**

#### Sending Documentation to VBA eFolder

Functionality from within the **Clinical Documents** tab allows the user to use **File-&gt;Send to VBA eFolder** menu option or the user can choose **Send to VBA eFolder** from the **Report Builder** window after the **Build** button has been selected.

1. Once **Send to VBA eFolder** has been selected, the user is prompted with a confirmation message of, “Are you sure you want to send document(s) to the VBA record of Veteran Name?” (Figure 2-212)
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-212. Transmit to VBA eFolder Prompt.

1. After selecting **Yes** , to transmit, CAPRI will automatically validate the user’s PIV certificate.
2. After selecting **OK** on the CAPRI Certificate Selection window (Figure 2-213), the user will be prompted to enter their Personal Identity Verification (PIV) pin, select **OK** to continue transmission (Figure 2-214).
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-213. CAPRI Certificate Selection Window

**PIV PIN Authentication** : When presented, enter PIV PIN to authenticate and select **OK** to transmit. If **Cancel** is selected, the user will receive a “Transmission cancelled by user.” dialog message. (Figure 2-226)

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-214 PIV Authentication Screen

The following dialog box (Figure 2-215) is displayed about the successful status of transmission.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-215. VBA eFolder Transmission

#### Send to VBA eFolder Transmission Error Codes

If the transmission fails, an error message will display with an error code describing the failure and the status of the transmitting file to VBA eFolder

**Error code: 400 Bad Request** . This error indicates the server was unable to understand the attempted transmission.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-216 Transmission Error Code: 400 Bad Request

**Error code: 400 MTLS error** . This error indicates that the user is not authorized to access the resource.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-217 Transmission Error Code: 400 MTLS

**Error code: 401 Unauthorized User** . This error indicates the server did not receive a properly formed authorization token (JWT).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-218 Transmission Error Code: 401 Unauthorized User

**Error code: 403 File number error** . This error indicates the Veteran file number did not resolve.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-219 Transmission Error Code: 403 File Number

**Error code: 500 Series Server Error** . This error indicates the transmission encountered an issue when processing the request.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-220 Transmission Error code: 500 Series Server

**Error code: 900 Transmission Disabled.** This error message indicates that transmissions to eFolder are disabled for this site.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2--221 Transmission Error code: 900 Transmission Disabled

**Clinical Document Transmission Error** – **proxy transmission** . When a proxy transmission encounters a connection timeout, the tranmssion will retry up to five times. When failing after the fifth attempt, the transmission will result in error code 901 (Figure 2-223).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-222. Proxy Transmission Timed Out After 5 Attempts

**Error code: 901 Connection Timeout.** This error occurs when the transmission times out after five transmission attempts. The user should try to transmit again.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-223 Transmission Error code: 901 Timed Out

**Clinical Document Transmission Error – Proxy TLS** . When a proxy transmission encounters a TLS error, the tranmssion will retry up to five times. When failing after the fifth attempt, the transmission will result in error code 75788.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-224. Proxy TLS Transmission Retry

**Error code 75788:** Error message that is displayed if the error code is anything other than 400,401 or 500. This will be displayed if an unknown error code is received, typically after the Proxy TLS tranmission has timed out after 5 attempts.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-225 Transmission Default Error Code 75788

Transmission cancelled by user dialog message if cancelling during PIV authentication (Figure 2-226 Transmission Cancelled by User Dialog).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-226 Transmission Cancelled by User Dialog

**Error code 96260:** Socket error. Please contact local IT or enter a service ticket.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-227. Socket Error

The maximum size of a document(s) that can be transmitted is 15mb. If the document(s) that have been selected exceed this size the user is notified with the following message in.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-228. VBA eFolder Transmission File Size Error Prompt.

When users attempt to transmit a patient file to eFolder that is missing a claim of file number, the transmission will fail, and the error message below will be displayed. Users will need to manually upload the documents to eFolder (2.13.6 Manually Upload Documents to eFolder).

Error message: “CAPRI cannot transmit your documents to the VBA eFolder automatically because a claim number or alternate linking data cannot be located in VistA for this patient. As a result, you must upload the document(s) to the correct VBA eFolder manually by converting them into a PDF format and then uploading them via VBMS. See the CAPRI User Guide for more information.”

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-229 CAPRI Cannot Transmit Your Document Claim Number or Data not Located Error

#### Manually Upload Documents to eFolder

When the automatic transmission of clinical documents from CAPRI to VBMS eFolder is unavailable, users will need to manually upload the documents. For example, if a user's PIV authentication fails or a Veteran patient cannot be identified, the user will need to manually upload PDF versions of the clinical docs to the correct eFolder in VBMS

<!-- image -->

1. Select the Clinical Document to upload to eFolder. You can use Report Builder to select multiple documents that can be built into one (see section 2.8.2).
2. From the CAPRI menu, select **File** .
<!-- image -->
3. Select **Print** .
4. In the Print window under Name, select **Microsoft Print to PDF** .
<!-- image -->
5. Press **OK** .
6. Enter the file name and select **Save** .
7. The CAPRI Dialog window will appear with the message, “This report has been sent to the printer.” select **OK** .
8. Locate and open the PDF file.
9. Manually upload the PDF versions of the clinical document(s) to the corresponding VBMS eFolder. (For additional information on eFolder, please refer to the VBMS User Manual).

#### VBA eFolder Queue Button

When a DBQ fails to transmit to VBA eFolder, an XML file is saved and users will be able to transmit manually anytime using the **VBA eFolder Queue** button.

Click the **VBA eFolder Queue** button to view the lists of XML files that can be manually transmitted.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-230. VBA eFolder Queue Button.

The VBA eFolder Queue window displays. The user has the option to transmit one or transmit all. When the user selects one of the XML, information such as the Patient Name, the Originating Date, and Item Type is displayed. The Item Type will display the name of the DBQ. When the transmit succeeds, the file is deleted from the dialog and a status of “Transmission Completed” is displayed at the bottom of the window.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-231. Successful transmission.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-232. Status of Failed Transmission

If there are files that can be transmitted manually, the following message is presented to the user upon log on and before exiting the CAPRI application.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-233. Confirm Manual Transmission of Files

#### Get Docs from DAS - On the File Menu

**FILE|GET DOCS FROM DAS** has been created to allow CAPRI VHA users to retrieve and view documents from **central data store DAS** . The user **MUST** hold the **DVBA CAPRI GETDOCSFROMVLER** security key to access this functionality.

To access the GET DOCS FROM DAS:

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-234. CAPRI File Menu—Get Docs from DAS Selection.

The “ **RETRIEVE EXAM** ” window will be displayed and allow the CAPRI user to enter exam search criteria for documents to be retrieved.  The following search criteria are acceptable.

> **NOTE:** All queries must have a Date Range and at least one other search criteria.

- Patient SSN (must be 9 digits or 9 digits +P for Pseudo SSN) and Date Range
- VA Facility ID (exact match) and Date Range
- Physician (Not case sensitive and can be a partial match) and Date Range
- Exam Status (completed or rejected only) and Date Range
- Or multiple selection of the above and Date Range

Once the search criteria have been chosen click the “Search” button:

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-235. Retrieve Exam from DAS View.

The **“Retrieve Exam Documents from DAS”** window will be displayed with results from search criteria selected and allow the CAPRI user to open documents for the selected Veteran, by clicking on document and choosing “View Selected Document” button or by double clicking on the document. This window allows the user to sort by column and view the sort in Ascending or Descending order.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-236. Retrieve Documents from DAS View.

#### Validate the Patient Restricted List

A CAPRI Remote user can have restricted access to patient records they are authorized to view.

> **NOTE:** As determined by the Health Information Access (HIA) Office, the CAPRI application shuts down if the CAPRI remote user is unable to access their restricted patient list on the CLAIMS server. Prior to the CAPRI application being shut down, the CAPRI Remote user receives a notification that their restricted patient list could not be accessed, and they are provided HIA contact information. The CAPRI Remote user also sees the restricted patient list that could not be accessed displayed in the lower left status bar of the CAPRI mail form.

#### Performing HIA Search

While in the CAPRI application, navigate to the Tools drop down menu (Figure 2-237). Select the option HIA Search. Click the S elect File button to have the Open dialog window display.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-237. CAPRI Tolls Menu—HIS Search Selection.

Note 1: There are two methods that create the same results. The preferred method is to click the Select File button. The other method is by clicking on the main menu Process (Upper left) and then selecting the Import (Figure 2-238) option.

Note 2: The legend at the bottom of this screen reflects the possible MVI search results.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-238. HIA Search View.

In the Open dialog window (Figure 2-239) select the Patient Restricted list file, .csv file type.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-239. HIA Search—File Selection.

Ensure that the correct selected file name appears in the HIA Selected File: window displayed in (Figure 2-240). If the HIA Selected File name is NOT correct, click on the S elect File button to choose a different file name. If correct click the V erify button near the bottom of the screen. Click the C ancel button (next to the Verify button) to return to the CAPRI home screen.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-240. HIA Search—Cancel to Close.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-241. HIA Search Results within the MVI database.

#### Print Option Method

Select the File menu option, then select the Print option shown in the following (Figure 2-242).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-242. HIA Search File Menu—Print Selection.

The Print Setup dialog box appears. Select your Printer, Paper type, and Orientation choice, and then click the OK button. (Figure 2-243)

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-243. Print Dialog Window.

The following (Figure 2-244) displays the fixed formatted print results.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-244. HIA Search Print Results.

#### Export Method Option

Select the Process menu option, then select the HIA Export Result option shown in the following figure:

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-245. HIA Search Process Menu—HIA Export Result.

The Save As dialog box displays, (Figure 2-246). Select your Save in location to store your .csv file. Next accept the default File Name: field or create a file name of your choice ensuring the file extension remains .csv. The Save as type: option is set only for .csv file format. Click the Save button to complete the file save option.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-246. HIA Search Export File Save.

To view the result of your .csv file, navigate to the location that you saved the file. By default, if you have the MS Excel application installed, you can double click on the file name and the file opens in an Excel format shown (Figure 2-247). If changes are made to your open Excel file and it is desired to save them and keep the file in the .csv file type, select **File** from the main menu, then select the **Save A** s option. The **Save As** dialog box displays, then select the **Save as type** drop down list. Select option: **CSV(MS-DOS) (*.csv)** and click on the **Save** button to complete. You can also save your new file in an Excel file format by following the same method but save it as a standard Excel format.

You can also right-click on the file name and select open with the Notepad application. Notepad displays the results in a common delimited format. Once the file is opened in Notepad and if changes are made that you desire to retain, select **File** and click **Save** and it saves at the existing location as a .csv file type.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-247. HIA Search File Save Results.

#### Edit Remote User Site Access

The Remote User Sites Editor window, found on the Help menu, is available to users with @ or U level FileMan access (Figure 2-248). This tool allows a CAPRI administrator to change the remote sites for specific CAPRI users, either limiting their site list or providing the full list of VHA VistA systems to the user. This list is presented to the administrator when CAPRI is started in remote mode.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-248. Remote User Sites Editor.

The **User:** list is generated from the Claims system, which runs on the Forum hardware hosted by the VHA Office of Information in Silver Spring, MD. The DVBAB RESTRICTED LIST PATIENTS remote procedure is used to restrict unauthorized patient record access.

To begin the process, the administrator selects a name from the **User:** column. The second column is updated with the user’s site access privileges. Selecting **Yes** for **Show all sites?** displays every available VistA site on the user’s **Authorized Sites:** list. If the user should have access to only a limited number of sites, then the administrator should select **No** for this radio group and update the user’s allowed sites under **Authorized Sites** .

One user’s site list can be copied to other users. This is useful when a known group of users accesses the same sites. This function is accomplished using the third column of the utility.

- **Step 1-** A user is selected and the list of sites in the second column is updated.
- **Step 2** - After the user is set up correctly, the **Use Selected as Parent Clone** button is selected. This user is then listed in the Parent Clone edit box.
- **Step 3** - Each user who should have the same site list is selected, and the Add Selected to Child Clone List button is clicked.
- **Step 4** - After the list is set up correctly, the process is completed by selecting the Start Cloning button.

#### Consolidated Remote Reports

**Consolidated Remote Reports,** found on the Help menu, is available to users of CAPRI when in remote mode. This report tool was primarily created for the Tiger Team to run reports on multiple VistA accounts at the same time. To use it, the user clicks the sites on which the report is to run in the **Available Connections** list box. The user then enters stop and start dates, chooses a report type, and clicks **Run Selected** . This dialog box has its own **Print** button which should be used instead of selecting Print from the File menu and printing from the main CAPRI interface. See (Figure 2-249).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-249. Consolidated Remote Reports Screen.

#### Audit Trail Download Utility

Audit Trail download utility, found on the Help menu, is available to users with @ or U level FileMan access and who have been provided an additional secondary menu option on the Claims system (Figure 2-250). This tool is used by VBA to download CAPRI audit data for internal reporting. Although a user may see this option on the menu, the secondary menu option that allows it to run may not be provided. The utility automatically continues at the last point where it stopped if the same filename is selected as during a previous session. If a new filename is created, the utility starts from scratch and downloads every entry in the VistA Claims system database.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-250. CAPRI Audit Trail View.

#### Purge Site Transmission Data

The menu option **Purge Site Transmission Metrics** is under Tools on the CAPRI menu bar. Purge Site Transmission metrics is a tool used to remove stored transmission data in CAPRI. This menu option is only visible to users who have the **DVBA CAPRI METRICS PURGE** security key assigned.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-251 Purge Site Transmission Location

The user will be presented with a date selection window. Select a purge date and press **OK** .

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-252 Purge Date Selection

The user will be presented with a confirmation window to continue or cancel the purge. The transmission metrics will be purged from the selected date and all prior dates.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-253 Purge Metrics Confirmation

The user will receive the following message if the purge was successful.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-254 Purge Successful Confirmation

### Miscellaneous CAPRI Functionality

#### Edit Exam List Parameters

The menu option **Edit Exam List Parameter (MAS)** is under Tools on the CAPRI menu bar. Exam List Parameter (MAS) is a tool used by sites to define which exams are performed at a division. When an exam request is made via the C&amp;P exams tab, the exam list displays exams as performed or not performed in a division according to this definition (Figure 2-255).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-255 CAPRI Tools Menu—Edit Exam List Parameters

The Security Key **DVBA CAPRI EXAM LIST EDIT** should be assigned to the MAS ADPAC, C&amp;P Clinic Clerk, or other individual who is responsible for maintaining the list of C&amp;P exams performed at the site.

The user, who has the Security Key above or programmer access, should see **Edit Exam List Parameter (MAS)** under the Tools menu option after logging into the CAPRI GUI executable.

If the user does not have option context DVBA CAPRI GUI, it should be assigned to their secondary menu.

For users who do not hold this Security Key or who have @ FileMan access, the option is not displayed. When a user clicks **Edit Exam List Parameter (MAS)** , the dialog box in Figure 2-256 displays. Parameters that are set in this window are displayed in the **Add a New C&amp;P Exam** dialog box.

Modifications were made to the “Edit Exam List Parameters” form/option to add functionality to control the disabling of a Routing Location for a 2507 C&amp;P Exam Request and a 7131 Request (Release of Information) (Figure 2-256).

If a new division(s) has/have been added, the next time a user uses this option it sets the new division(s) to inactive by default for both 2507 C&amp;P Exam Requests and 7131 Requests. CAPRI users can manually activate the new division(s) by unchecking the ‘Completely Inactive this Division’s checkbox.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-256. Edit Exam List Parameters Screen.

To turn off a division so it is not available for user selection, the user selects the division on the left of the screen then selects one or both of the **Completely Inactivate this Division…** boxes for **C&amp;P Exam Requests** and/or **7131 Requests** in the **Edit Exam List Parameters (MAS)** window as shown in (Figure 2-257). A textbox is provided for comments relating to a specific division.

When selecting **Edit Exam List Parameters (MAS)** for the first time, CAPRI automatically finds the site’s divisions and activates all exams for each division in a default list. The user is then able to add new lists or modify the default list that was created.

To enable a specified group of users to order certain exams, the user may create a custom list by selecting the **Add New** button in the **Edit Exam List Parameters (MAS)** window.

When the user selects an exam list defined for a division, the Edit Selected button is enabled. Selecting this button triggers the display of the screen below (Figure 2-257) to enable users to modify the list of exams and add comments about a particular list or exam.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-257. Edit Exam List Parameters Dialog Window.

The checkmarks in the checkboxes next to the exam name indicate that the exams are activated.

Users can modify (turn off) the default as well as the custom exam list by clearing the check marks from the checkboxes next to the exams they wish to turn off. This causes the exam to display in red in the **Add a New Exam** dialog box, indicating that the exam is not performed at the particular facility.

If exams are turned off via future DVB patches, they no longer display in the **New Exam** dialog box.

If a patch releases a new exam, it is shown to all users until someone uses **Edit Exam List Parameter (MAS)** to turn it off.

Comments may be added for the exam list in the section labeled **Comment for this list of exams** . Users can place comments, reminders, or notes about an exam in the text box labeled, **Comment to be displayed when this exam is selected** . Selecting the exam brings the textbox into view.

Additionally, a functionality has been added that allows facilities to disable the ability to submit C&amp;P Exam Request and/or 7131 Requests by disabling the “Add a New Request” button specific to each of those requests (Figure 2-258). When selecting to disable the ability to submit either of the requests, a pop-up confirmation message will appear (Figure 2-259 &amp; Figure 2-260). Click **Yes** to confirm the action.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-258. Facility Control – Disable Request Submissions

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-259. Confirm Disabling Ability to Add New C&amp;P Exams

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-260. Confirm Disabling Ability to Add New 7131 Requests

Once confirmed, the user will see a confirmation message on the respective tab that the site is no longer accepting that specific request (Figure 2-261 &amp; Figure 2-262). The “Add a New Request” button will now be disabled.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-261. Site Not Accepting C&amp;P Exams Notification

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-262. Site Not Accepting 7131 Requests Notification

#### Joint Longitudinal Viewer (JLV) Tab

> **NOTE:** Training materials for the JLV system are available on the JLV Resources SharePoint site

The Joint Longitudinal Viewer (JLV) tab has replaced the VistAWeb tab as part of the GUI 193.12 update in preparation of the VistAWeb end of lifecycle. The Joint Longitudinal Viewer tab supplies a button that launches the JLV URL in an external browser while keeping patient context. The JLV provides an integrated read-only view of health data from all VA, and VA community partner sites where the Veteran or Service member has received care.

Prior to the release of DVBA*2.7*240, JLV launched patient context information within Internet Explorer. This functionality has been updated to launch patient context information within the user’s default browser as Internet Explorer will be retired and no longer supported as of June 15, 2022.

When selecting the JLV button, the user will be presented with a CAPRI alert message, informing them that the JLV will launch and attempt to load patient information in addition to a reminder to close the JLV browser tab when the user switches to a new patient within CAPRI. (Figure 2-264)

When switching patients within CAPRI, users will be receiving an alert prompt informing them that JLV was previously launched and to close the previous JLV browser tab to protect patient context information, to keep from accidentally entering wrong information into the previous patient into the current patient’s data. (Figure 2-265)

When selecting, Select Patient and entering the Patient ID, after selecting the patient and moving from the C&amp;P exams tab to the Joint Longitudinal Viewer tab and selecting the JLV Joint Longitudinal Viewer button, if the patient selected does not have an ICN number assigned, you’ll receive a CAPRI Dialog alert, “Unable to locate the required information to load this patient within JLV” (Figure 2-266)

Users will receive an alert, “Could not identify JLV URL” when unable to communicate with the JLV website. As this alert is rare, it may be possible that you’ve lost internet connection. (Figure 2-267)

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-263. Joint Longitudinal Viewer (JLV) launching button

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-264 Remember to close the JLV tab when switching to a new patient

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-265 JLV was previously launched, close the previous JLV browser tab to protect patient information

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-266 Unable to locate patient ICN

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-267 Could not identify JLV URL

> **NOTE:** In order to maintain patient context information between CAPRI and Joint Longitudinal Viewer, remember to close the JLV browser each time a patient or site is switched.

### Set Up Options

#### Printer Set Up

CAPRI has Windows printer functionality. The user can generate printouts using the default printer selection.

1. The user selects Printer Setup from the File menu (Figure 2-268).
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-268. CAPRI File Menu—Printer Setup Selection.

1. CAPRI displays the current default printer. If the user has more than one printer, selecting the down arrow at the end of the printer name displays a list of available printers. To permanently change the default printer, the user must contact IRM or use the printer’s applet under the Windows control panel.

#### Display Properties

Display properties of CAPRI can be modified using Windows DPI settings.

1. Right click on an empty part of your Desktop and select Display Settings.
<!-- image -->
2. Scroll down to Scale and Layout.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-269. Display Settings Page

1. Click the dropdown and select the appropriate Scale and Layout value.  This will change the size of text, apps and other items
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-270. Scale and Layout

To set Accessibility, go to the Start/Control Panel/Ease of Access/Ease of Access Center (Figure 2-271).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-271. Windows Ease of Access View.

Selecting Change Font takes the user to the standard Windows Fonts dialog box that can also be opened via Start/Control Panel/ Appearance and Personalization /Fonts.

**Patient Lookup Keyboard Sensitivity** (Figure 2-271) adjusts the amount of time CAPRI waits after a keystroke before searching for the patient on the Patient Selector screen. The user should not change this from the default setting of 500 milliseconds unless there are difficulties.

If the user makes any changes to these settings and wants to undo the changes, then the user should select **Restore Original Font/Keyboard Settings** (Figure 2-271).

#### Change Forwarding Address

Change Forwarding Address on the Tools menu allows the user to specify an Exchange email address for forwarding MailMan messages and CAPRI reports (Figure 2-272). The user can find an Exchange email address by opening the Address Book in the default email application and searching for the desired name. After the user finds the name and right-clicks it, Properties is selected. Then, the user should go to the Email Addresses tab. Local IRM staff should be contacted if the user has difficulty identifying the email addresses.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-272. CAPRI Tools Menu—Change Forwarding Address Selection.

#### Forwarding Email Address

This email address is used for automated AMIE responses to certain activity such as cancelled C&amp;P exam requests and completed C&amp;P exam request bulletins. The responses are emailed back to the user if there is an email address specified (Figure 2-273).

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-273. Email Forwarding Address Dialog Window.

#### Help Menu

For most users, this menu only has two items, About (Figure 2-275) and CAPRI C&amp;P Template Training Website. Other items only show up for users who have been assigned these options.

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-274. CAPRI Help Menu.

#### About

The **About CAPRI** screen displays the CAPRI version number (Figure 2-275). The user can also see this number on the CAPRI splash screen at start-up or when switching sites when using CAPRI in remote mode.

The user can select **OK** (Figure 2-275), to close the **About CAPRI** screen. The user should have this information available when reporting a problem.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-275. About CAPRI View.

#### Training Website

Selecting **CAPRI C&amp;P Template Training Website** takes the user to the CAPRI website with video training materials.

#### Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons

**Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons** allows a user to add/inactivate/reactivate 2507 Cancellation Reasons. This option is available from the **HELP** menu.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-276. CAPRI Help Menu—Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons Selection.

This option will only be available to **CAPRI-Remote (national) users** who are assigned security key “ **DVBA CAPRI EXAMCANC** .”

**SECURITY KEY ALLOCATION** **:**

**DVBA CAPRI EXAMCANC** – must be assigned to a user that will need access to the **“Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons”** option.

**MANAGE EXAM CANCELLATION REASONS** option functionality:

1. From the **HELP** menu choose “ **Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons** ” option
2. The “Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons” window will be displayed
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-277. Manage Exam Cancellation Screen.

1. To **Inactivate** an Active reason, choose the reason in the **Active list** and click the “ **Inactivate** ” button. This will move the active reason chosen to the **Inactive list** column.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-278. Exam Cancellation Screen—Active Selection.

1. To **Activate** an Inactive reason, choose the reason from the **Inactive list** and click the “ **Activate** ” button. This will move the inactive reason chosen to the **Active list** column.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-279. Exam Cancellation Screen—Inactive Selection

1. To add a cancellation reason, click the “ **Add New Cancellation Reason** ” button

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-280. Exam Cancellation Screen—Add New Cancellation Reason.

1. Enter the new cancellation reason in the text field. Cancellation reason must be **3-40** characters in length.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-281. Exam Cancellation Screen—Cancellation Reason.

1. To **save** the cancellation reason just entered, click “ **Save Cancellation Reason** ” button. Once saved, the reason entered will be automatically added to the “ **Active** ” list column.
2. If you **do not** want to **save** the cancellation reason, click “ **Cancel** ” button. Reason will “not” be added to either the Active or Inactive list columns.
3. To **EXIT** the “ **Manage Exam Cancellation Reasons** ” window click the “ **Close** ” button or “ **X** ” to close the window.

#### Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons

**Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons** allows a user to add/inactivate/reactivate 2507 Insufficient Reasons. This option is available from the **HELP** menu.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-282. CAPRI Help Menu—Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons Selection

This option will only be available to **CAPRI-Remote (national) users** who are assigned security key “ **DVBA CAPRI EXAMCANC** .”

**SECURITY KEY ALLOCATION** **:**

**DVBA CAPRI EXAMINSUFF –** must be assigned to a user that will need access to the **“Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons”** option.

**MANAGE EXAM INSUFFICIENT REASONS** option functionality:

1. From the **HELP** menu choose “ **Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons** ” option
2. The “Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons” window will be displayed
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-283. Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons.

1. To **Inactivate** an Active reason, choose the reason in the **Active list** and click the “ **Inactivate** ” button. This will move the active reason chosen to the **Inactive list** column.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-284. Exam Insufficient Reasons—Active Selection.

1. To **Activate** an Inactive reason, choose the reason from the **Inactive list** and click the “ **Activate** ” button. This will move the inactive reason chosen to the **Active list** column.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-285. Exam Insufficient Reasons—Inactive Selection

1. To add an insufficient reason, click the “ **Add New Insufficient Reason** ” button
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-286. Exam Insufficient Reasons—Add New Insufficient Reason.

1. Enter the new insufficient reason in the text field. Insufficient reason must be **1-75** characters in length.
<!-- image -->

Figure 2-287. Exam Insufficient Reasons—Insufficient Reason Field.

1. To **save** the insufficient reason just entered, click “ **Save Insufficient Reason** ” button. Once saved, the reason entered will be automatically added to the “ **Active** ” list column.
2. If you **do not** want to **save** the insufficient reason, click “ **Cancel** ” button. Reason will “not” be added to either the Active or Inactive list columns.
3. To **EXIT** the “ **Manage Exam Insufficient Reasons** ” window click the “ **Close** ” button or “ **X** ” to close the window.

#### Manage Routing Reasons

**Manage Routing Reasons** allows a user to add/inactivate/reactivate Routing Reasons. This option is available from the **HELP** menu.

<!-- image -->

Figure 2-288. CAPRI Help Menu—Manage Routing Reasons Selection.

This option will only be available to users with assigned security key “ **DVBA CAPRI REROUTEREASONS** .”

**SECURITY KEY ALLOCATION** **:**

There are no new Security Key allocations.

## Troubleshooting and Error Messages

> **NOTE:** In all cases, please print the error message for your local IRM staff.

### CAPRI Not Installed in VistA

The user receives the message in (Figure 3-1), if the VHA Medical Center has not loaded the VHA half of the CAPRI software. The user should contact local IRM staff after receiving this message.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-1. CAPRI Not Loaded on Server Alert.

### CAPRI GUI Option Not Assigned to User in VistA

The user receives the message in (Figure 3-2), if the VHA medical center has not assigned the CAPRI option to RO users. The user must contact local IRM staff upon receiving this message.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-2. CAPRI User Access Alert.

### VistA Server Down

The user receives the message in (Figure 3-3), or a similar one such as WSAETIMEDOUT, when there are performance issues in the VA Wide Area Network (WAN), if a server is down, or if a server was not restarted after being down. When this happens, AMIE II may or may not connect, depending on the exact problem. The user should test the AMIE II connection.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-3. VistA Server Unavailable Alert.

### VistA Limits Ability to See Patient Records

The user receives the message in (Figure 3-4), if the medical facility made local permission modifications in VistA that prohibit users from seeing the contents of patient records.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-4. User Permission Restricted to View Alert.

### Network Problems

The user receives the message in (Figure 3-5) if the connection to the medical facility is lost unexpectedly. The user should try to connect to the medical facility again.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-5. Connection to Medical Facility Unavailable Alert.

### Institution File in VistA has Been Locally Modified

The user may receive the message in (Figure 3-6. Incorrect Regional Office List Alert.) when requesting exams, requesting 7131s, or entering new patients if the VHA medical facility has an incomplete or incorrect Regional Office list in their system.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-6. Incorrect Regional Office List Alert.

### Too Many Invalid Attempts at Access Code / Verify Code

The user receives the message in (Figure 3-7. Multiple Invalid Login Attempts Alert.), if the user attempted to log on and entered the wrong Access Code / Verify Code combination three or more times.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-7. Multiple Invalid Login Attempts Alert.

### Multiple Sign-Ons

The user may receive the message in (Figure 3-8. Multiple Sign-on Alert.), if the user did not log out of CAPRI correctly or if the session was unexpectedly disconnected.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-8. Multiple Sign-on Alert.

### General Error Message

The user may receive the message in (Figure 3-9), for several reasons. Upon receiving this message, the user should cancel the current task, close CAPRI, and sign on again. If the user receives this error message again, local IRM staff should be contacted for assistance.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-9. CAPRI Login Error Message.

### Warning Message Displayed for GUI Version of CAPRI

A warning message is displayed for GUI version of CAPRI that does not match the current version of the VistA patch.

**Sample Warning Message 1:** For GUI versions of the CAPRI application that do not match the current version of the VistA patch, a warning message displays that the software does not match what is running on the Vista server.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-10. Sample Software Version Error Message 1

**Sample Warning Message 2:** If the user fails to update the client software within the seven (7)-day grace period, message below displays:

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-11. Software Version Error Message 2

Pressing OK at the screen above will display the message below:

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-12. Software Version Error Message 3

### Error Message for 7131 Request

When placing a new 7131 Request, CAPRI requires that at least one checkbox is marked, and data has to be entered into the "Comments" memo box. The following error message displays if no checkbox is selected or no Comment is entered.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-13. 7131 Request Message 1

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-14. 7131 Request Message 2

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-15: 7131 Request Message 3

### Error Message on Enterprise Search

Date of Birth is currently one of the required fields when performing an Enterprise Search on REMOTE CAPRI.  The following error message displays when no Date of Birth is entered.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-16. Enterprise Search Screen

The following message displays when a user tries to connect to a site that he is currently connected to from Enterprise Search.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-17. Enterprise Search Error Message 1

The following message displays when a user selects a non VAMC entity or the entry selected is a non-VistA (i.e. Cerner) site from the list of Treating Facilities.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-18. Enterprise Search Error Message 2

### Error Message on CAPRI Edit Exam List Parameters (MAS) Option

CAPRI requires at least one division to be active in the EDIT EXAM LIST PARAMETERS (MAS) in CAPRI. The following message displays when a user tries to inactivate the last active division.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-19. Edit Exam List Parameter Screen

### Error Message on Other Facilities Visited

CAPRI displays the following message when attempting to switch to a non VAMC entity or a non-VistA (i.e. Cerner Station Number 200CRNR) site from the Remote Sites indicating that the entry selected is not a VA treatment facility containing patient records.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-20. Other Facilities Visited Button

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-21. Remote Sites Screen

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-22. Remote Sites Error Message.

### Incompatible DBQ Error Message

Due to the upgrade of CAPRI to Delphi Rapid Application Development (RAD) Studio 10.2 (also known as XE10) a number of DBQs have been upgraded to be compatible. When a user attempts to open or create a DBQ that is not compatible with CAPRI GUI 220.2.7, the following error message is displayed.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-23. DBQ Error Message

### Permanent Failure DBQ Transmission Error Message

When a permanent failure occurs during a DBQ transmission, CAPRI will not allow these DBQs to be retransmitted as there was a failure which cannot be resolved. Users who receive this error will be prompted to contact the Service Desk.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-24. DBQ Permanent Failure Error

### DBQ Transmission Error Message

Users will receive the following error message when the DBQ exam result fails to transmit (Figure 3-25). The transmission will automatically attempt to complete a successful transmission (Figure 3-26). After five failed attempts, users will receive a failed transmission error message (Figure 3-27. DBQ Transmission Failure Error). To retransmit, reference 2.13.7 VBA eFolder Queue Button.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-25. DBQ Transmission Error

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-26. DBQ Retransmission Attempt Message

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-27. DBQ Transmission Failure Error

### NEW PERSON file missing SSN – at Local Site

When launching CAPRI, if the user is missing their SSN at local site, they will be prompted with the following error message (Figure 3-28) informing them to submit a ServiceNow ticket or to contact support. After selecting OK, CAPRI will shut down and prevent users from working in CAPRI until the issue is remediated.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-28 CAPRI has detected user’s SSN is missing at local New Person file.

### NEW PERSON file missing SSN – at Remote Local Site

When attempting to connect to a remote site, if the site’s new person file is missing the user’s SSN, the user will receive the following message informing the user to submit a ServiceNow ticket or to contact support to remediate the issue.

Selecting OK on the message (Figure 3-29) will display the CAPRI Remote site list and allow the user to select another site to continue work on a different patient. If the user selects Cancel (Figure 3-30) CAPRI will shut down.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-29 CAPRI has detected NEW PERSON file at local site is missing user’s SSN.

<!-- image -->

Figure 3-30 Select New Site

| Print New Notices of Discharge                          | Print New Notices of Discharge                                                             |

| Ad Hoc Health Summary                                   | Health Summaries – Adhoc Report                                                            |
| Admission Report for Service Connected Veterans         | CAPRI Reports – Admission Report for SC Veterans                                           |
| AMIS 290 for the Regional Office                        | CAPRI Reports – AMIS 290                                                                   |
| Beneficiary Information Status Inquiry                  | 7131 Request – Status Inquiry                                                              |
| Cancel C&P Requests (all exams)                         | C&P Exams – View/Edit Selected Request – Cancel ALL Exams                                  |
| Cancel C&P Requests (selected exams)                    | C&P Exams – View/Edit Selected Request – Select Exam – View Exam – Cancel This Exam        |
| Detailed Inpatient Inquiry                              | Reports – Detailed Inpt. Inquiry                                                           |
| Discharge Report (A&A, Pension, Service Connected, All) | CAPRI Reports – Discharge Report (A&A, Pension, SC, All)                                   |
| Edit 7131 Remarks                                       | 7131 Request – View/Edit Selected Request                                                  |
| Edit C&P Request Information                            | C&P Exams – View/Edit Selected Request                                                     |
| Edit Patient Address Information                        | Admin – Edit Address Information                                                           |
| Enter a C&P Exam Request                                | C&P Exams – Add a New Request                                                              |
| Full Patient Profile MAS                                | Reports – Patient Profile MAS (Full)                                                       |
| HS Health Summary Menu                                  | Health Summaries tab                                                                       |
| Incompetent Veterans Report                             | CAPRI Reports – Incompetent Veterans Report                                                |
| INPT Detailed Inpatient Inquiry                         | Reports – Detailed Inpt. Inquiry                                                           |
| INQ Patient Inquiry                                     | Reports – Pt. Inquiry                                                                      |
| Inquiry for C&P Requests                                | C&P Exams – Status Inquiry                                                                 |
| Insufficient Exam Report                                | CAPRI Reports – Insufficient Exam Report                                                   |
| LAB Interim Report                                      | Clinical Documents – Lab tab                                                               |
| NHE Network Health Exchange Options                     | CAPRI Remote Data View                                                                     |
| NOTE Print Document Menu                                | Clinical Documents – Notes tab                                                             |
| OP Operation Report                                     | Reports – Surgery Report                                                                   |
| PDX Patient Data Exchange                               | CAPRI Remote Data View                                                                     |
| Pending C&P Exams Report                                | CAPRI Reports – Pending C&P Exams                                                          |
| Pending Form 7131 Requests Report                       | CAPRI Reports – Pending Form 7131 Requests                                                 |
| Print C&P Final Report (Manual)                         | Print C&P Final Report (Manual)                                                            |
| Print Exam Check List for RO                            | CAPRI Reports – C&P Exams Checklist                                                        |
| Re-admission Report                                     | CAPRI Reports – Re-Admission Report                                                        |
| Regional Office 21-day Certificate Printing             | CAPRI Reports – 21 Day Certificate Printing                                                |
| Regional Office Patient Inquiry                         | Reports – Pt. Inquiry                                                                      |
| REG View Registration Data                              | Reports – View Registration Data                                                           |
| Report for Pension and A&A                              | CAPRI Reports – Discharge Report, select Pension or A&A                                    |
| Reprint a 21-day Certificate for the RO                 | CAPRI Reports – Reprint a 21 Day Certificate (can run with or without a patient selected)  |
| Reprint a Notice of Discharge                           | CAPRI Reports – Reprint a Notice of Discharge (can run with or without a patient selected) |
| Reprint C&P Final Report                                | Re-Print C&P Final Report(s)                                                               |
| Request for 7131 Information                            | 7131 Request – Add a New Request                                                           |
| XRAY Radiology Reports for VARO                         | Clinical Documents – Imaging tab                                                           |

## ## 6 CAPRI Locations for Medical Evidence

> **NOTE:** This list is not meant to be all-inclusive in terms of providing the evidentiary requirements for the evaluation of disabilities. Rather it is meant to provide helpful hints for locating commonly used types of medical evidence.

Table 5-1. Musculoskeletal (DC 5000, 5100, 5200, 5300) Location Table.

| **What do I need?**               | **Where to go in CAPRI**             |
|-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| History and Physical Examinations | C&P Exam, Notes, Discharge Summaries |
| Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)  | Imaging Tab                          |
| X-rays                            | Imaging Tab                          |
| Arthrograms (X-ray dye)           | Imaging Tab                          |
| Arthroscopies                     | Imaging Tab                          |
| Range of Motion Studies           | Notes Tab, Consults Tab              |
| Surgical Procedures               | Reports Tab/Surgery Report           |
| Surgical Pathology                | Labs Tab/Anatomic Pathology          |
| Prescribed medications            | Meds Tab                             |

Table 5-2. Eye (DC 6000) Location Table.

| **What do I Need?**                                                                | **Where to go in CAPRI**             |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| History and Physical Examinations                                                  | C&P Exam, Notes, Discharge Summaries |
| Ophthalmology reports                                                              | Notes Tab, Consults Tab              |
| Eye exams w/Snellen test (visual acuity) and Goldmann Perimeter Chart (field test) | Notes Tab, Consults Tab              |
| Surgical Procedures                                                                | Reports Tab/Surgery Report           |
| Surgical Pathology                                                                 | Labs Tab/Anatomic Pathology          |
| Prescribed medications                                                             | Meds Tab                             |

| **What do I Need?**                                       | **Where to go in CAPRI**             |
|-----------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| History and Physical Examinations                         | C&P Exam, Notes, Discharge Summaries |
